summary: The formal_conjectures dataset contains 4557 problems with 2,571 Lean 4 conjectures, of which 1,029 remain open (marked `sorry`). Available activity data is sparse and appears inconsistent: only problem fc0000 has issue-resolution detail (1 resolved, 0 open), while the proof-status metrics report a uniform 100 issues across quarters q1-q10, which suggests aggregate or placeholder counters rather than per-problem traction.
problems: fc0000: 1 resolved issue titled 'Proof: getCategoryStatsMarkdown' — appears to be tooling/reporting rather than a mathematical proof
Proof status shows a flat 100 issues for every quarter (q1-q10), an unrealistic uniform distribution indicating possible placeholder, capped, or mis-aggregated data
No per-problem open-issue breakdown is available beyond fc0000, making it impossible to identify genuine mathematical traction
1,029 open problems (sorry-marked) lack individual activity signals in the provided context
highlights: fc0000 shows successful issue resolution, demonstrating the pipeline can close items end-to-end
Large, well-categorized corpus (number theory, combinatorics, analysis, algebra) provides strong coverage for prioritized work
Clear open/resolved tagging via `sorry` markers enables straightforward identification of the 1,029 open targets

suggested_todos.title: Audit the proof-status counters — the uniform 100-issues-per-quarter pattern likely indicates a capped or placeholder metric and should be fixed for reliable reporting
Generate per-problem open-issue breakdowns for the 1,029 sorry-marked conjectures to surface real traction and solvability signals
Verify whether fc0000's 'getCategoryStatsMarkdown' issue is tooling vs. a math proof, and separate infrastructure issues from mathematical conjectures in tracking
Triage open conjectures by domain (number theory, combinatorics, etc.) to identify low-complexity candidates likely solvable within a week
Establish an activity metric (recent edits, comments, commits) per problem to enable future traction-based prioritization
